title: Linai's Enchanted Forest Friends
description: Linai drifts through a magical forest and discovers a radiant fairy and a wonderfully funny dwarf.

acts.approachText: Drifting softly between the ancient mossy trunks, Linai suddenly catches a shimmer of light ahead — a tiny fairy hovers among the ferns, wings catching the dappled sunlight like stained glass. Linai slows immediately, her body relaxing outward, her glow becoming cleaner and calmer. Her eyes widen softly, and gentle sparkles bloom inside her translucent form. She drifts closer by degrees, pulled in by curiosity and delight at once, her vapor trails settling into long, quiet ribbons beneath her. The fairy notices Linai and tilts her head, uncertain whether to stay or flutter away into the canopy. Linai hovers attentively, trying to show she means only wonder and friendship — but she must find just the right way to approach so the fairy does not startle.

choices.label: Pulse a soft warm glow toward her
choices.outcomeText: Linai dims her usual brightness to a gentle amber warmth and pulses it outward in slow, even waves — like a lantern breathing. The fairy's wings still. She tilts her head the other way, then drifts one inch closer. Then another. Then she lands on the tip of Linai's vapor trail, laughing in tiny bell-tones. Linai's internal patterns bloom into a cascade of sparkles, her body expanding with barely-contained delight as she rises just a little higher than necessary. Both of them hover there in shared silence for one perfect beat — and then Linai looks toward the viewer with an expression that says 'I knew,' before the sparkles spill outward in every direction.

choices.label: Surge forward to say hello quickly
choices.outcomeText: Linai's excitement gets the better of her and she surges forward in a bright rush — her trails snapping outward, her glow flaring. The fairy shrieks a tiny shrike-bell shriek and vanishes in a burst of golden dust, leaving only a swirling leaf behind. Linai stops mid-float, trails drooping, both eyes angling outward toward the viewer with a flat 'you see this?' expression. She lets out a quiet, deflated hum and settles lower, reconsidering.

acts.approachText: Continuing deeper into the forest, Linai rounds a wide oak and nearly phases straight into a very short, very round dwarf who is standing in the middle of the path juggling three pine cones while singing a completely made-up song about cheese. He has an enormous red hat, a beard braided with tiny flowers, and boots that are slightly too big. He does not notice Linai at all — he is far too busy performing for an audience of one very unimpressed squirrel. Linai hovers behind him, her eyes wide with delighted surprise, her body pulsing brighter as particles scatter outward. She wants to get his attention and share in the fun, but the dwarf is deep in his one-man show and the squirrel is already leaving.

choices.label: Gently envelop one pine cone mid-air
choices.outcomeText: As the dwarf tosses a pine cone upward, Linai silently drifts into its arc and envelops it in a soft glowing bubble — the pine cone hangs suspended, glowing faintly gold. The dwarf reaches up, grabs air, blinks, and slowly turns around. He finds Linai hovering there, one pine cone floating inside her like a tiny trophy, her eyes curved into the smuggest crescents imaginable. There is one beat of absolute silence. Then the dwarf bursts into the loudest, most delighted laugh in the forest, slapping his knees, his hat tumbling off. Linai releases the pine cone with a cheerful pulse, her body expanding with sparkles, and the two of them drift and waddle side by side down the path together — the squirrel watching them go with the same unimpressed expression.

choices.label: Phase through him to appear in front
choices.outcomeText: Linai decides the fastest way to introduce herself is to simply drift through the dwarf and materialize in front of him with a cheerful glow. The dwarf feels a sudden cold tingle, freezes mid-juggle, and all three pine cones bonk him on the hat in quick succession. He spins around, then forward, eyes wide, beard puffed out with static. He stares at Linai. Linai stares back. His hat slowly slides down over his eyes. Linai's trails droop apologetically. She angles both eyes toward the viewer — flat, patient, acknowledging — and begins drifting back around to try a different approach.
